economic_notes: First Nation owns 33.3% of A.C. Forestry Ltd. and 33.3% of A.C. Realty Ltd. (government business enterprises). COVID-19 impacted operations including office closures, travel restrictions, and cancellation of events. First Nations Trust gaming revenues expected to be negatively impacted by temporary closure of Saskatchewan Indian Gaming Authority casinos.
notes: Audit is qualified for two reasons: (1) auditors were denied access to financial information of A.C. Forestry Ltd. and A.C. Realty Ltd. (33.3% owned GBEs), so carrying value is nil and revenue of $261,664 from A.C. Forestry is included but unverified; (2) no budget was prepared or approved by Chief and Council for the fiscal year, which is a departure from PSAS. Comparative 2019 figures were restated due to prior period adjustments including reclassification of restricted cash, correction to accounts receivable from trust funds, and correction to investment accounting. CMHC housing consolidated as subsidiary. No remuneration schedule included in the financial statements provided. Restricted cash of $1,027,584 for capital projects and $136,549 for CMHC reserves totalling $1,164,133. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $9,889,619 + CMHC $329,984 + Agency Chiefs Child and Family Services $771,024 + National Health and Welfare $122,156 + Agency Chiefs Tribal Council $432,779 = $11,545,562 less ISC recovered funding note suggests net ISC is already $9,889,619; total_government_transfers set to ISC + CMHC + ACFS + NHW + ACTC = 11545562 but adjusted to reflect only transfers from governments. OSR includes land leases, rent, A.C. Forestry, SIGA, Community Development Corporation, interest, and other revenue.
